card.title: mazer: icon-quality 2026 visual target
card.summary: Version and hash-pin the approved Mazer icon authority across source references, delivery assets, runtime material diagnostics, and route-aware visual proof.
card.objective: Keep the supplied 2026 icon, every browser/PWA/shortcut derivative, and the game's cyber-arcade material target source-linked and machine-verifiable.
acceptanceCriteria: App icon assets are saved and wired for browser, Vercel, and app surfaces.
Game materials adopt the icon's crisp cyan/green glow, dark glass, and high-contrast arcade geometry.
Atlas keeps the icon reference available for later Stripe/link/auth surfaces.
discoveries: Both preserved Atlas icon references are binary-identical while the shipped canonical 1024px asset is a separately derived, hash-pinned delivery artifact.
Runtime material diagnostics can enforce the icon target version and canonical SHA without regenerating or mutating approved artwork.
nextActions: Preserve the versioned icon-quality authority and focused delivery/runtime guards.
Use the same source-linked contract for later Mazer brand surfaces rather than copying unversioned assets.
evidence: Focused brand/material/scene verification passed 3 files / 56 tests; architecture passed 5 files / 18 tests plus the firewall.
Full npm run verify passed 47 files / 355 tests plus a 224-module Vite/PWA build with 34 precache entries.
Authenticated menu, Options, play, and pause captures passed at 405x958 and 1440x900 with zero console warnings/errors or page errors.

entry.headline: Icon-quality target implementation and proof complete
completed: Established mazer-icon-quality-target-v1 with exact source and delivery asset lineage.
Published target version and canonical SHA through the shared cyber-arcade runtime material diagnostics.
Guarded binary hashes, dimensions, HTML/manifest/shortcut wiring, and completion-marker truth.
Completed clean authenticated mobile and desktop route-aware proof and post-work review.
discovered: No artwork regeneration, gameplay change, topology change, account mutation, or Supabase mutation was required.
next: Transfer the exact card to Completed after guarded review journal readback.
